bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My most important issue is issueD (0.4 weight), followed by issueC (0.3), issueB (0.2), and issueA (0.1). For all issues, valueF gives the highest utility of 1.0. I'll open with all valueF to maximize my utility at the start of the negotiation.

The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueA, which gives me minimum utility of 0.2 across all issues = total ~0.2), while I opened with all valueF (maximum utility = 1.0). The opponent's message about urgency is fake talk — we're only at t=0.02, very early. I should stay close to my optimal bid. I'll concede slightly on issueA (lowest weight = 0.1) but hold firm on the most important issues (issueD, issueC).

The opponent is using fake urgency tactics ("closing fast", "critical window") but we're only at t=0.04 - very early in the negotiation. Their bids are all valueA across all issues, which gives me minimum utility (0.2 per issue weighted = 0.2 overall). I started at maximum utility (all valueF), then slightly conceded on issueA. The opponent hasn't moved at all. I'll stay firm near my optimal bid since we have plenty of time. I'll make a minor concession on issueA (my least important issue) but hold firm on issueD and issueC (my most important issues).

I should hold firm near my optimal position. I'll make a small concession on issueA (lowest weight for me) while holding firm on the more important issues, and maybe slightly concede on issueB to show willingness to negotiate.
